reasoning: The echo serialize($data) at line 227 inside _export() is gated by two independent guards: (1) current_user_can('edit_theme_options') in init() at line 58, which requires administrator-level privilege in standard WordPress; and (2) wp_verify_nonce($_REQUEST['cei-export'], 'cei-exporting') at line 171 inside _export() itself, using a per-user session-bound nonce issued only to users who have already loaded the Customizer UI (itself restricted to edit_theme_options holders). An attacker without that capability cannot obtain a valid nonce and cannot reach the echo. The data echoed is the site's own theme mods, customizer option values, and custom CSS — none attacker-controlled. The cei_export_option_keys filter is the only theoretical expansion point, but it requires a third-party plugin to register sensitive keys; the plugin itself registers none. This is the plugin's intentional, designed export feature for administrators. No sanitizer or guard was missed.
